statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

statistics.name: turnovers
statistics.description: The number of times a player loses possession to the other team.

statistics.description: The average number of assists a player or team records per turnover

statistics.name: rebounds
statistics.description: The total number of rebounds (offensive and defensive).

statistics.name: defensiveRebounds
statistics.description: The number of times when the defense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot by the offense.

statistics.name: offensiveRebounds
statistics.description: The number of times when the offense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot.

articles.headline: Las Vegas visits New York following Wilson's 30-point game
articles.description: Las Vegas Aces (14-5, 10-4 Western Conference) at New York Liberty (12-8, 8-1 Eastern Conference)

categories.description: Las Vegas Aces @ New York Liberty

event.description: Las Vegas Aces @ New York Liberty

articles.headline: Scuffling Liberty call for more 'heart' after latest loss
articles.description: The Liberty are searching for answers after their fourth loss in five games, with star forward Breanna Stewart challenging her team to play with more heart.

articles.headline: Charles ties season high with 13 points as Valkyries down Liberty 76-67
articles.description: — Kaila Charles tied a season high with 13 points off the bench and the Golden State Valkyries held the New York Liberty to their lowest-scoring first half of the season en route to a 76-67 victory on Sunday night.

images.name: Golden State Valkyries vs. New York Liberty - Game Highlights

event.description: New York Liberty @ Golden State Valkyries

articles.headline: A'ja Wilson, Las Vegas Aces top Kamilla Cardoso, Chicago Sky
articles.description: Four-time MVP A'ja Wilson finished with 30 points and 15 rebounds Sunday as the Aces posted a 107-99 victory over Kamilla Cardoso and the Chicago Sky at United Center, Las Vegas' 14th win of the season and second in a row.

images.name: A'ja Wilson scores 30 vs. Chicago Sky
images.caption: A'ja Wilson scores 30 vs. Chicago Sky

articles.headline: Mystics win marathon matchup, beat Fire in record-tying 4 OTs
articles.description: Sonia Citron scored a career-high 32 points, including the basket that put her team ahead to stay with 21.4 seconds left, and the Washington Mystics held off the Portland Fire 124-123 on Sunday in four overtimes -- tying the longest game in WNBA history.

images.name: Michaela Onyenwere [600x400]
images.caption: Michaela Onyenwere, who scored a career-high 30 points, was among eight players who played 45-plus minutes Sunday, the most in a game in WNBA history.

images.name: Carla Leite can't get it to fall as Fire lose in 4OT to Mystics
images.caption: Carla Leite can't get it to fall as Fire lose in 4OT to Mystics

articles.headline: Mystics win a marathon matchup, topping Fire 124-123 in record-tying 4 OTs
articles.description: — Sonia Citron scored 32 points, including the basket that put her team ahead to stay with 21.4 seconds left, and the Washington Mystics held off the Portland Fire 124-123 on Sunday in four overtimes — tying the longest game in WNBA history.

images.name: Carla Leite can't get it to fall as Fire lose in 4OT to Mystics
images.caption: Carla Leite can't get it to fall as Fire lose in 4OT to Mystics

articles.headline: A’ja Wilson has a WNBA first with 30 points, 15 rebounds, 4 steals, 3 blocks in Aces' win over Sky
articles.description: — A’ja Wilson had 30 points, 15 rebounds, four steals and three blocks to become the first player in WNBA history to reach those marks in a game as the Las Vegas Aces beat the Chicago Sky 107-99 on Sunday.

images.name: A'ja Wilson scores 30 vs. Chicago Sky
images.caption: A'ja Wilson scores 30 vs. Chicago Sky

images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from Chicago Sky vs. Las Vegas Aces, 06/28/2026

articles.headline: Lynx coach Cheryl Reeve ties WNBA regular-season wins record
articles.description: Cheryl Reeve tied Mike Thibault's WNBA record for career coaching wins at 379 as the Minnesota Lynx beat the Dallas Wings 85-77 on Sunday.

images.name: Cheryl Reeve [600x600]
images.caption: Lynx coach Cheryl Reeve won for the 379th time as a WNBA coach Sunday, tying the league's all-time record.

images.name: Lynx go on the road and take down the Wings
images.caption: Lynx go on the road and take down the Wings

articles.headline: Howard and Miles score 21, Reeve ties WNBA coaching wins record as Lynx beat Wings 85-77
articles.description: — Natasha Howard had 21 points and 14 rebounds, Olivia Miles added 21 points and eight assists and Cheryl Reeve tied the WNBA record for career coaching wins as the Minnesota Lynx beat the Dallas Wings 85-77 on Sunday.

images.name: Lynx go on the road and take down the Wings
images.caption: Lynx go on the road and take down the Wings

articles.headline: Lynx go on the road and take down the Wings
articles.description: Lynx go on the road and take down the Wings

images.name: Lynx go on the road and take down the Wings
images.caption: Lynx go on the road and take down the Wings

articles.headline: Rookies Flau'jae Johnson and Awa Fam lead the Storm to a 105-90 victory over the Dream
articles.description: — Flau'jae Johnson had 24 points and 11 rebounds, fellow rookie Awa Fam scored 21 points and the Seattle Storm never trailed in a 105-90 victory over the Atlanta Dream on Saturday night.

images.name: Seattle Storm vs. Atlanta Dream - Game Highlights
images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from Seattle Storm vs. Atlanta Dream, 06/27/2026

articles.headline: Parker, Delle Donne, Reeve enter Women's Basketball Hall of Fame
articles.description: Candace Parker and Elena Delle Donne, former rivals who each won two WNBA MVP awards, and Cheryl Reeve, who has won four titles as head coach, have been enshrined in the Women's Basketball Hall of Fame.

article.headline: Shakira Austin hits winning free throws as Mystics spoil Tempo opener with 68-65 win
article.description: — Shakira Austin made four free throws down the stretch and the Washington Mystics held on to beat the Toronto Tempo 68-65 on Friday night, spoiling the Tempo's first game.

tracking.trackingName: WNBA_ One Play (Brittney Sykes scores first basket in Tempo history) 2026/05/08 ESHEET

article.story: TORONTO -- — Shakira Austin made four free throws down the stretch and the Washington Mystics held on to beat the Toronto Tempo 68-65 on Friday night, spoiling the Tempo's first game. Sonia Citron scored 26 points, Austin had 18 points and 11 rebounds, and Kiki Iriafen added 12 points and 16 rebounds for the Mystics. Austin made two free throws with 18 seconds left to put Washington ahead 66-65, then added two more with 11 seconds to play. Marina Mabrey scored 27 points and Brittney Sykes had 14 but the Tempo couldn’t overcome poor shooting. Toronto shot 5 for 25 from 3-point range and went 17 for 63 overall. Sykes connected on Toronto’s first shot of the game, giving the expansion team its first points, but came up empty on a 3 that would have put the Tempo in front with 14 seconds remaining. After Cassandre Prosper opened the game with a layup for Washington, the sellout crowd of 8,210 roared as Sykes connected on the first shot in Tempo history, a pullup jumper from the free-throw line. New Dallas Mavericks president and former Raptors executive Masai Ujiri, who is part of the Tempo ownership group, sat at center court. Also in attendance were current Raptors Immanuel Quickley and Sandro Mamukelashvili, former Raptor Serge Ibaka, and Canadian soccer star Christine Sinclair. Fans sang along to a song by Canadian country music star Shania Twain during a Karaoke Cam gimmick in the fourth quarter. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
